Description: AI generates structural metadata (IDs, hooks) before content, establishing referential integrity.
Implementation Strategy: Use schemas that enforce the generation of fields like regionId, seedId, deadledgerRef, luaModule, etc., as required fields.
Governing Component: chatdirector-envelope-v1.json; family-specific schemas (chatdirector-moodcontract-v1.json, chatdirector-eventcontract-v1.json, chatdirector-regioncontractcard-v1.json, chatdirector-seedcontractcard-v1.json)

Description: Provides predefined structural defaults to guide the AI and ensure a baseline level of correctness.
Implementation Strategy: Define chatDirectorSkeletonProfilev1 mapping archetypeId + objectKind + tier to default envelopes and partial bodies.
Governing Component: plan command embeds or points to a skeleton in the chatDirectorRequestv1

Description: Introduces a dedicated field to indicate the completeness of content.
Implementation Strategy: Add a contentStatus field to schemas with an enum such as placeholder, draft, final.
Governing Component: Core authoring schemas that carry content fields (e.g., chatdirector-envelope-v1.json and family-specific contract schemas)

Description: Replaces informal placeholder text with a controlled set of valid tokens.
Implementation Strategy: Define enum fields for sensitive or optional content, for example PLACEHOLDER_EXAMPLE_ONLY, REDACTED_FOR_TIER1, or EMPTY.
Governing Component: JSON Schema enum keywords on specific placeholder-capable fields, as referenced by CHATDIRECTOR Modules 7 and 15

Description: Restricts placeholders to specific, non-critical fields to preserve core structural integrity.
Implementation Strategy: Design schemas to have separate, clearly named fields for AI placeholders (for example, placeholderNarrative) versus human-facing content (for example, flavorText).
